commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed for runtime override product policy and current-main SQL/generated merge-result safety; this is not a narrow autonomous repair candidate.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain defect was found in the latest diff; the channel runtime override authorization remains a maintainer product/merge-risk decision. (sha=f95564ac062f81ff1eb60a7ff0b254bfb78cd44d)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds durable agent activity messages, a coalesced web preamble UI, self-message bubbles/alignment, composer runtime controls, channel-runtime API/storage, tests, docs, and regenerated embedded web assets.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a current-main bug report. The contributor supplied video proof for the visible UI and PR-body terminal/e2e evidence for the runtime API behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed for runtime override product policy and current-main SQL/generated merge-result safety; this is not a narrow autonomous repair candidate.

target.title: feat(web): cohesive agent-activity preamble + composer runtime controls + self-message bubbles
target.body:
## Summary

Builds on #24 (ephemeral `agent.progress` activity lines) to make agent activity **durable and cohesive** in chat history. Where #24 shows live activity that vanishes after the TTL, this PR turns it into a persistent, collapsible preamble block that lives in the transcript and reads as one cohesive unit with the agent's final answer.

This is the full vision in one PR, as requested in review on #24.

## What's included

- **Durable agent activity (API).** `agent.progress` activity is persisted as durable rows (schema + store types + queries) instead of being purely ephemeral, so it survives reload and history fetch.
- **Inline rendering with accent, badge, and toggle.** Agent activity renders inline in the message stream with a distinct accent, an activity badge, and an expand/collapse toggle.
- **Coalesced preamble block.** Consecutive activity for a turn coalesces into a single collapsible `PreambleBlock`, instead of N separate lines.
- **Commentary + per-tool rows, interleaved.** The block interleaves agent commentary with per-tool rows; **tool-call detail is collapsed by default** and expandable on demand (`tool-detail.ts`).
- **Final pill labeled with visibility state.** The closing pill reflects the block's current state (expanded / collapsed).
- **One cohesive card.** The preamble and the final answer are boxed together into a single cohesive card, visually distinct from a plain text message (different treatment for preamble vs. final text).
- **Profile toggle.** New profile setting to control commentary/tool-call visibility.

## Files

- API durable layer: `schema.sql`, `sqlite.go`, `storedb/*`, `store/types.go`
- New UI: `components/messages/PreambleBlock.svelte`, `MessageRow.svelte`, `MessageGroup.svelte`
- New libs: `lib/chat/agent-activity.ts`, `lib/chat/tool-detail.ts`, `lib/types.ts`
- Settings: `components/profile/ProfileSettingsModal.svelte`
- Styles: `styles/messages.css`, `styles/base.css`
- Embedded dist rebuilt to match (`webassets/dist`)

## Verification

Run against the post-merge `main` (this PR rebased cleanly onto the merged #24):

| Step | Result |
|---|---|
| `pnpm typecheck` (web) | exit 0 |
| `pnpm build:web` | exit 0 |
| `pnpm embed:webassets` | exit 0 (dist reproducible) |
| `go build ./...` | exit 0 |
| `go test ./internal/httpapi -run 'AgentActivity\|AgentProgress'` | **PASS** (5.4s) |

No e2e test removal, no lockfile churn. Embedded `dist` regenerates reproducibly (content-hash filenames stable).

---

## Update — bundled scope

Per review direction this PR now carries the full composer / runtime-controls increment on top of the preamble work, and has been merged up to current `main` (resolves the #26 browser-notifications overlap in `ChatApp.svelte` / `ProfileSettingsModal.svelte` — union merge, nothing dropped).

Added on top of the preamble block:

- **Self-message bubbles.** The current user's own messages render in an accent-tinted bubble that mirrors the agent delivery bubble's weight (7×11 padding, 11px radius, 3px rail) via a single theme-tied token (`--user-bubble-soft` = `color-mix` of `--accent`), so the dark-theme override flows automatically. Bubble hugs content (`fit-content`) so alignment is visible. Bots never match the current-user id, so an agent group is never self-styled (even in a DM with the agent).
- **Configurable left / right alignment.** New "Your message alignment" select in Account settings (Left default / Right). Persisted client-side (`clickclack:user-align:v1`) and applied as a `data-user-align` root attribute; `messages.css` mirrors the self group (avatar gutter, header, bubble) with no prop drilling.
- **Composer runtime controls.** Model picker (provider → model → reasoning cascade) and a context-window meter with an expandable detail popover (context %, cache hit, usage), wired to a channel-runtime store.
- **Agent-responding status.** Compact "agent is responding…" line above the composer, driven by any unfinalized tracked turn line.
- **Dead preamble title removed.** The "Preamble" heading (markup + `.preamble-title` CSS) is gone; the header now reads chevron + state pill + action.

## Demos

**Live composer + preamble walkthrough** — recorded against the stock validation instance. Every message, every preamble line, and the collapse/expand is real UI reacting to realtime socket events; nothing is a frozen frame with caption overlays.

![ClickClack composer + preamble live demo](https://github.com/ragesaq/clickclack/raw/media/composer-demo-video/.proof/video/clickclack-composer-demo.gif)

The capture runs a full turn end to end: the composer types and sends the user message, the agent works live in-channel (commentary + per-tool rows stream in one at a time), the trail folds into the **COLLAPSED** gold preamble pill, expands to show the full trail, collapses again, then the composer model picker + context-window meter, and the same flow on mobile.

Full-quality MP4 (1600×1000 · 30 fps · 83 s · 2.1 MB): https://github.com/ragesaq/clickclack/raw/media/composer-demo-video/.proof/video/clickclack-composer-demo.mp4

Feature coverage in this PR:

1. **Agent-activity preamble** — collapsed pill → expand reveals the coalesced, boxed tool rows interleaved above the final answer → collapse.
2. **Self-message bubbles + alignment** — terracotta self-bubbles flip left ↔ right, plus the Account-settings alignment control.
3. **Composer model picker** — provider / model / reasoning cascade.
4. **Context-window meter** — detail popover (context %, cache hit, usage).

## Verification (post-merge)

| Step | Result |
|---|---|
| `npm run typecheck` (web) | exit 0 |
| `npm run build` (web) | exit 0 |
| embed dist + `go build ./apps/api/cmd/clickclack` | exit 0 |
| merged up to `main` (#26) | conflicts resolved (union), no behavior dropped |
| live smoke on :8100 | self-bubble token + browser-notif both served |


---

## Follow-up — review fixes (Copilot + Clarity)

Two items raised in review, resolved in commits `4733232` + `f0e59ef`:

- **`turn_id` contract enforcement (Copilot, `server.go`).** Both create paths (`createMessage` + `createDirectMessage`) now run through a single shared choke point, `resolveMessageKind`, which validates `turn_id` against `kind`. An ordinary `kind="message"` carrying a non-empty `turn_id` is rejected `400 ErrTurnIDNotAllowed` (fail-closed, not silently cleared); agent activity kinds may carry one. The `400` body surfaces the sentinel text so the client sees *why* it failed, and a test asserts that on both the channel and DM paths. The `TurnID` field doc now states the asymmetry explicitly (must be empty for ordinary messages, optional for activity kinds).
- **Go coverage gate.** The lift to `86.5%` (gate `85.0%`) comes from a new `runtime_test.go` that covers **this PR's own composer-runtime handlers** (`get`/`put`/`patchChannelRuntime`, previously `0%`) plus the `turn_id` contract above. This is on-point coverage of the feature this PR introduces, not coverage padding of unrelated surface.


---

## Update — runtime cleanup + ClawSweeper review fixes (`f95564a`)

This closes the three findings from the ClawSweeper review (demo seed, stale-clear, CSRF-routing), adds input validation on the override write path, and lands one real bug fix that a new in-app test caught.

### Review findings resolved

- **[P1] Production demo runtime seed removed.** `seedDemoChannelRuntime` and its `onMount` call are gone; channels with no real bridge-stamped runtime row now render the unknown/empty state instead of fabricated model/context/cache values. (`rg seedDemoChannelRuntime` → none.)
- **[P2] Stale runtime data cleared on channel switch.** `refreshSelectedChannelRuntime` clears the store (`replace({})`) before the fetch, guards the result with a serial counter + selected-channel re-check, and `loadChannelRuntime` now returns an explicit empty record (`{}`) for empty channels rather than leaving the previous channel's values in place. A transient fetch failure preserves the cleared state (unknown), never another channel's data.
- **[P2] Override writes route through the CSRF-aware API helper.** `persistRuntimeOverride` and `loadChannelRuntime` now go through the shared `api()` helper, which adds `X-ClickClack-CSRF: 1` on unsafe methods, instead of a raw `fetch`. A normal cookie-authenticated browser session can now persist a runtime override (previously rejected `403`).

### Added on top of the review

- **Override field validation at the API boundary** (`apps/api/internal/httpapi/runtime.go`). `patchChannelRuntime` now validates input before persisting: `thinking` must be one of `off|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh|adaptive` (empty clears the override); `model` is length-bounded (≤256, no control characters). A channel member can no longer persist an arbitrary or oversized string that the bridge would hand to a gateway session. Bridge `PUT` (bot-token + `agent_activity:write`) is unchanged.
- **Context-meter render-gate (real bug fix).** A new E2E that switches channels *in-app* (sidebar click, not `page.goto`) caught a stale-carryover: switching to a channel with no runtime data left a `Context ?/?` meter pill mounted even though the model picker cleared. `ComposerContextMeter` now renders the pill only when the channel reports real `context_used/limit`, matching the component's documented contract. Picker and meter now clear together on switch.

### Proof

Redacted terminal/test proof for the network/API behavior the review asked for:

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| `go test ./apps/api/internal/httpapi/...` | **PASS** — cookie PATCH without CSRF → `403`; cookie PATCH with `X-ClickClack-CSRF: 1` → `200` with `override_*` persisted; bridge/effective fields unchanged; invalid `thinking`/oversized `model` rejected |
| web `pnpm typecheck` | exit 0 |
| Playwright e2e (full suite, 5 spec files) | **38/38 passed** — incl. the new in-app sidebar-click channel-switch stale-carryover test |
| web build + embedded `webassets/dist` | rebuilt, reproducible |

The full-page persistence + empty-state behavior is exercised by the e2e suite; the CSRF accept/reject contract is proven server-side by the Go test (the production picker→`api()` path adds the header automatically).

### Policy note (unchanged, restated)

Channel-scoped runtime override via PATCH (`messages:write` + channel membership) is **product-approved** for this PR: a channel member can set the model/reasoning the agent uses *in that channel*. Bridge-stamped effective runtime stays bot-only (`PUT`, `agent_activity:write`). This split is documented in `docs/RUNTIME_CONTROLS_INDEPENDENT_PLANE.md`.
target.author: ragesaq

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

| live smoke on :8100 | self-bubble token + browser-notif both served |


---

## Follow-up — review fixes (Copilot + Clarity)

Two items raised in review, resolved in commits `4733232` + `f0e59ef`:

- **`turn_id` contract enforcement (Copilot, `server.go`).** Both create paths (`createMessage` + `createDirectMessage`) now run through a single shared choke point, `resolveMessageKind`, which validates `turn_id` against `kind`. An ordinary `kind="message"` carrying a non-empty `turn_id` is rejected `400 ErrTurnIDNotAllowed` (fail-closed, not silently cleared); agent activity kinds may carry one. The `400` body surfaces the sentinel text so the client sees *why* it failed, and a test asserts that on both the channel and DM paths. The `TurnID` field doc now states the asymmetry explicitly (must be empty for ordinary messages, optional for activity kinds).
- **Go coverage gate.** The lift to `86.5%` (gate `85.0%`) comes from a new `runtime_test.go` that covers **this PR's own composer-runtime handlers** (`get`/`put`/`patchChannelRuntime`, previously `0%`) plus the `turn_id` contract above. This is on-point coverage of the feature this PR introduces, not coverage padding of unrelated surface.


---

## Update — runtime cleanup + ClawSweeper review fixes (`f95564a`)

This closes the three findings from the ClawSweeper review (demo seed, stale-clear, CSRF-routing), adds input validation on the override write path, and lands one real bug fix that a new in-app test caught.

### Review findings resolved

- **[P1] Production demo runtime seed removed.** `seedDemoChannelRuntime` and its `onMount` call are gone; channels with no real bridge-stamped runtime row now render the unknown/empty state instead of fabricated model/context/cache values. (`rg seedDemoChannelRuntime` → none.)
- **[P2] Stale runtime data cleared on channel switch.** `refreshSelectedChannelRuntime` clears the store (`replace({})`) before the fetch, guards the result with a serial counter + selected-channel re-check, and `loadChannelRuntime` now returns an explicit empty record (`{}`) for empty channels rather than leaving the previous channel's values in place. A transient fetch failure preserves the cleared state (unknown), never another channel's data.
- **[P2] Override writes route through the CSRF-aware API helper.** `persistRuntimeOverride` and `loadChannelRuntime` now go through the shared `api()` helper, which adds `X-ClickClack-CSRF: 1` on unsafe methods, instead of a raw `fetch`. A normal cookie-authenticated browser session can now persist a runtime override (previously rejected `403`).

### Added on top of the review

- **Override field validation at the API boundary** (`apps/api/internal/httpapi/runtime.go`). `patchChannelRuntime` now validates input before persisting: `thinking` must be one of `off|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh|adaptive` (empty clears the override); `model` is length-bounded (≤256, no control characters). A channel member can no longer persist an arbitrary or oversized string that the bridge would hand to a gateway session. Bridge `PUT` (bot-token + `agent_activity:write`) is unchanged.
- **Context-meter render-gate (real bug fix).** A new E2E that switches channels *in-app* (sidebar click, not `page.goto`) caught a stale-carryover: switching to a channel with no runtime data left a `Context ?/?` meter pill mounted even though the model picker cleared. `ComposerContextMeter` now renders the pill only when the channel reports real `context_used/limit`, matching the component's documented contract. Picker and meter now clear together on switch.

### Proof

Redacted terminal/test proof for the network/API behavior the review asked for:

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| `go test ./apps/api/internal/httpapi/...` | **PASS** — cookie PATCH without CSRF → `403`; cookie PATCH with `X-ClickClack-CSRF: 1` → `200` with `override_*` persisted; bridge/effective fields unchanged; invalid `thinking`/oversized `model` rejected |
| web `pnpm typecheck` | exit 0 |
| Playwright e2e (full suite, 5 spec files) | **38/38 passed** — incl. the new in-app sidebar-click channel-switch stale-carryover test |
| web build + embedded `webassets/dist` | rebuilt, reproducible |

The full-page persistence + empty-state behavior is exercised by the e2e suite; the CSRF accept/reject contract is proven server-side by the Go test (the production picker→`api()` path adds the header automatically).

### Policy note (unchanged, restated)

Channel-scoped runtime override via PATCH (`messages:write` + channel membership) is **product-approved** for this PR: a channel member can set the model/reasoning the agent uses *in that channel*. Bridge-stamped effective runtime stays bot-only (`PUT`, `agent_activity:write`). This split is documented in `docs/RUNTIME_CONTROLS_INDEPENDENT_PLANE.md`.
